channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Audiomack became an unlikely Spotify competitor
item.description: Kendrick Lamar. Drake. Lady Gaga. The charts of music streaming services pretty much all look the same these days, with familiar names dominating the top spots—except on up-and-coming Spotify competitor Audiomack. The current No.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Google Cloud unveils new AI Protection security tools, no matter which model you use
item.description: Google Cloud's new AI Protection suite offers features designed to mitigate risks across AI workloads and data.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Utah becomes first state to pass app store age verification law
item.description: Utah passed a bill on Wednesday that will require app stores to verify users' ages and receive parental consent for minors to download applications, making the Beehive State the first in the country to pass such a measure. The bill, titled the App Store Accountability Act, now heads to Utah Gov.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon's Prime Video Tests AI Dubbing on Select Titles
item.description: Prime Video is testing a new AI-assisted feature for dubbing its movies and shows to make its content accessible to even more of its over 200 million strong global subscriber base.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: Super Micro Computer pushes ahead with plans for new San Jose campus
item.description: Supermicro Computer is pushing ahead with a dramatic expansion of its San Jose footprint by constructing a third tech campus.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: First Fujifilm GFX100RF images leaked in build-up to expected reveal – here’s what they tell us about the unique premium compact camera
item.description: Leaked images seemingly reveal the top and rear of the rumored Fujifilm GFX100RF, the anticipated 100MP medium-format compact.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tres requisitos imprescindibles a la hora de contratar a proveedores de IA
item.description: Dos años de experimentación con la IA generativa pueden haber dado lugar a varios casos de uso valiosos pero, en este período, los líderes de TI también han aprendido que no hay que lanzarse a ciegas a esta nueva tecnología en rápida evolución.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Your older Pixel phone just got a performance and camera boost thanks to Google's new software update
item.description: We're getting more details about software upgrades rolling out to Pixel phones old and new this week.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Salesforce takes on hyperscalers with Agentforce 2dx updates
item.description: Salesforce’s updates to its agentic AI offering — Agentforce — released this week could give the CRM software provider an edge over its enterprise application rivals and hyperscalers including AWS, Google, IBM, Service Now and Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Utah Passes Landmark Legislation Requiring App Stores to Verify Users' Ages - The Latest in Children's Online Safety
item.description: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users' ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps, sparking a debate over who should be responsible for verifying ages.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: JetZero teams with Delta to take the first blended-wing body commercial jetliner closer to reality
item.description: It’s tough enough bringing the first major redesign of a commercial jetliner to fruition—especially when it looks more like something out of The X-Files than a travel brochure.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Asymmetric AI warfare in the era of cheap intelligence
item.description: Artificial intelligence innovation is at the forefront of this advancement and presents the necessity to detect emerging threats. The post Asymmetric AI warfare in the era of cheap intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Tariffs will doom Trump’s attempt to pump crypto
item.description: For cryptocurrency investors, Donald Trump represents a great hope. In the libertarian paradise where tokens on blockchains pave the future of the financial system instead of banks, lenders, and brokerages, Trump could even be a prophet.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Musk to speak to Senate Republicans on Wednesday
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk, the moving force behind the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), will meet with Senate Republicans on Wednesday to answer their questions and concerns about his aggressive efforts to shrink the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Google leans further into AI-generated overviews for its search engine
item.description: Google is updating its ubiquitous search engine with the next generation of its artificial intelligence technology as part of an effort to provide instant expertise amid intensifying competition from smaller competitors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A cheaper YouTube Premium Lite plan just rolled out in the US – but you’ll miss out on these 4 features
item.description: YouTube has announced that its cheaper Premium Lite plan is getting a full rollout in the US– with other countries getting it later "this year". Here's how it compares to full Premium.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Ancient humans made tools from animal bones 1.5 million years ago
item.description: A new study says early humans were regularly using animal bones to make cutting tools 1.5 million years ago.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge denies Musk request to block OpenAI conversion to for-profit company
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday rejected Elon Musk’s request to block OpenAI from becoming a for-profit company, finding the tech billionaire failed to show he was likely to succeed on the merits of the case. However, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This new dating app is bringing a Pokémon Go approach to romance
item.description: Ever miss the thrill of the 2016 Pokémon Go craze? A new anti-swiping dating app has come to fill that void, but instead of cute Pokémon you can catch yourself a hot date. These days, trying to find a partner IRL, whether at a bar or reluctantly joining a singles running club, is no easy task.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: USDA’s automations taking some of the burden out of HR work
item.description: Brian Mohr, USDA’s the enterprise automation program manager, said over the past year, the agency developed 15 new automations mostly for HR processes. The post USDA’s automations taking some of the burden out of HR work first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft rolls out updated Copilot app to Windows 11 testers
item.description: Microsoft has begun pushing out a new version of its generative AI (genAI) Copilot application to users testing new builds of Windows 11. The app has an updated interface with a panel on the right listing the history of queries that have been posed, and the responses on the left.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tariffs for now won’t impact IT organizations
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is so far not cause for alarm, according to one analyst.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Palo Alto expands secure private 5G network partnerships
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is looking to expand the role SASE plays in securing private 5G networks by collaborating with additional partners to offer end-to-end communications protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: What to expect from the AI leader
item.description: No company has both driven and benefited from AI advancements more than Nvidia. Last year, Nvidia’s GTC 2024 grabbed headlines with its introduction of the Blackwell architecture and the DGX systems powered by it. With GTC 2025 right around the corner (it runs March 17- 21 in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Your iPhone has a hidden folder eating up storage space without you even knowing
item.description: iPhones have a hidden folder that eats up storage without you knowing it. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some tricks to free up storage on your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Lawrence Livermore’s Brian Spears, Judy Hill on achieving unprecedented AI advancements
item.description: Powerful computing platforms let Lawrence Livermore evolve artificial intelligence to ultimately benefit government and Americans, lab’s top AI researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World
